{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the behavior of an SQP method. For the initial point from part (c), show that the linearized constraints associated with the constraint-violation model\n\\[\nm_k(p)=\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal{E}} |c_i(x_k)+\\nabla c_i(x_k)^T p|+\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal{I}} [c_i(x_k)+\\nabla c_i(x_k)^T p]^-\n\\tag{18.56}\n\\]\nare inconsistent, taking into account the constraints \\(s_1\\ge 0\\) and \\(s_2\\ge 0\\). Hence show that the SQP subproblem (18.11) is inconsistent, and therefore the constraints in the SQP subproblem must be relaxed."}
